title: Astronomy for Mortals
subtitle: Where infinity becomes understandable
author: Wordsus Team
description: A fascinating journey through the cosmos in 365 daily lessons. From the solar system to the far reaches of the observable universe, discover how space-time works, the stars and the great mysteries of astronomy explained in a simple way.

chapters.title: What is astronomy? An introduction to the cosmos
chapters.description: We begin our stellar journey by defining what exactly astronomy is, its fascinating history, and why observing the night sky captivates us so much.

chapters.title: The Sun: Our star, the heart of the solar system
chapters.description: Discover how our mother star works. We explore its composition, temperatures and how its enormous energy makes life on Earth possible.

chapters.title: The Moon: Our only and inseparable natural satellite
chapters.description: Learn the secrets of our natural satellite. We analyze its mysterious phases, the craters that adorn its surface and its influence on the tides.

chapters.title: Black holes: The light devourers of the universe
chapters.description: We demystify the most famous monsters in the cosmos. Learn what black holes are, how they form when stars die, and why nothing escapes.

chapters.title: Rocky planets: Close neighbors in our system
chapters.description: A detailed tour of Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. We analyze their solid surfaces, their atmospheres and what makes them planets so unique.

chapters.title: Gas giants: Majestic colossi of the solar system
chapters.description: We explore the immensity of Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune. Learn the amazing secrets of its dense atmospheres, rings and its numerous moons.

chapters.title: Constellations: The ancient figures that guide the night
chapters.description: Learn to identify the most famous constellations in the night sky. Discover their beautiful myths and how to use them to orient yourself among the stars.

chapters.title: Fascinating exoplanets: New worlds beyond the sun
chapters.description: We analyze the search for planets outside the solar system. Learn the techniques to discover them and which ones have a great chance of being alive.

chapters.title: Modern telescopes: Giant eyes looking into the sky
chapters.description: We review the operation of the instruments that revolutionized astronomy, from simple optics to enormous space observatories.

chapters.title: Asteroids and meteorites: The wandering rocks of the galaxy
chapters.description: We differentiate these ancient space debris. Understand what happens when they enter Earth's atmosphere and if they pose a real threat.

chapters.title: The Milky Way: A detailed walk through our spiral house
chapters.description: We travel through our gigantic galaxy. We explore its spiral shape, its dense central core and the incredible number of stars it contains.

chapters.title: The light year: Understanding the enormous distances of space
chapters.description: We explain how we measure the immensity of the universe. Discover why when looking at certain distant stars, we are literally peering into the past.

chapters.title: Majestic comets: Ancient snowballs in space
chapters.description: Accompany these frozen nomads in their orbit. Learn about its main components and how they form their spectacular and brilliant tails in the sun.

chapters.title: The Big Bang: The incredible explosion that caused all this
chapters.description: We go back billions of years to narrate the absolute beginning of the universe. We explain how matter, light and time suddenly emerged.

chapters.title: Bright nebulae: Wonderful birthplaces of stars
chapters.description: Delve into immense clouds of cosmic gas and interstellar dust. Contemplate these nurseries where stars are born with beautiful shapes and colors.

chapters.title: Red dwarfs: The most common stars in our galaxy
chapters.description: Get to know the most abundant and long-lived stars in the cosmos. We explain why they are so small, cold and essential for searching for habitable planets.

chapters.title: Dark matter: The great and invisible mystery of the universe
chapters.description: We address one of the greatest enigmas of science. We analyze how we know that invisible matter exists and how it manages to keep galaxies together.

chapters.title: The force of gravity: The glue that holds the cosmos
chapters.description: Discover the main force that shapes the universe. We easily explain how the most massive bodies manage to curve space and time around them.

chapters.title: Supernovas: The most violent explosions in the entire cosmos
chapters.description: Witness the last gasp of massive stars. Learn how these powerful and immense explosions seed space with all the heavy elements.

chapters.title: Andromeda Galaxy: Our immense and distant royal neighbor
chapters.description: We travel to the enormous galaxy closest to our Milky Way. We analyze its colossal great size and its inevitable and incredible future collision with us.

chapters.title: Total eclipses: The perfect dance of shadows in the sky
chapters.description: Understand the mechanics behind these beautiful astronomical events. We teach you why exactly they occur and how you can observe them safely.

chapters.title: Space junk: The worrying artificial terrestrial ring
chapters.description: We examine the thousands of fragments of inactive satellites orbiting the Earth. Learn about the current risks of the artificial ring and the possible cures.

chapters.title: Pulsars and neutron stars: The lighthouses of the universe
chapters.description: Get closer to the densest and most extreme stellar remnants. We explain how they spin vertiginously, emitting constant pulses of radiation like lighthouses.

chapters.title: Wormholes: Theoretical shortcuts through the cosmos
chapters.description: We delve into theoretical physics to talk about the strange bridges in space-time. Find out if it will actually be possible to use them to travel soon.

chapters.title: The dwarf Pluto: The endearing icy rock in the confines
chapters.description: We revisit the rich history of our ancient ninth planet. We discuss why he was reclassified and the amazing recent data we've discovered.

chapters.title: The expanding universe: The mysterious stellar receding
chapters.description: Discover why all the stars in the cosmos move away from each other. We explain the redshift and the enigmatic force known as dark energy.

chapters.title: Dark energy: The invisible accelerator of the vast universe
chapters.description: We delve into the strange constant engine that accelerates cosmic expansion. Learn about the hypotheses of this energy that dominates most of the cosmos.

chapters.title: The Voyager probes: Our furthest human travelers
chapters.description: We celebrate the pioneering mission of great exploration. We talk about his journey through outer planets, his famous golden disk and the final exit from the system.

chapters.title: Giant radio telescopes: Listening to the echoes of the cosmos
chapters.description: We explore how astronomers observe invisible frequencies of light. Understand how these large antennas pick up distant galaxies and radio waves.

chapters.title: The multiverse theory: Are there other parallel worlds?
chapters.description: We address an extremely amazing topic in cosmology. We discussed the incredible theoretical odds that this universe is just one among many others.
